content: backdrop-filter blur became Baseline 2024 (Sept 2024) — broadly supported; iOS Safari historically needed -webkit- prefix and still has layer-compositing quirks. Critical for camera-as-canvas: backdrop-filter only blurs content painted behind the element up to the nearest backdrop-root. Any ancestor with opacity<1, filter, mask, will-change:backdrop-filter, or transform becomes a backdrop root, so child backdrop-filter blurs only content INSIDE that ancestor not behind it. Therefore the <video> camera layer MUST sit OUTSIDE any opacity<1 wrapper, and each HUD overlay must be a direct child of the fixed app shell (no translucent scrim wrapping the HUDs) — otherwise blur samples the wrong layer. Translucent HUD bg (rgba ~0.35) is mandatory; opaque bg means no blur effect visible. GPU: backdrop-filter is expensive; 3+ simultaneous blurred overlays over live video is real framerate risk on mid-range mobile — cap concurrent blurred overlays, prefer will-change:backdrop-filter + contain:layout paint.
